cameraPrompt.azimuthLabelZh: 对象左前方机位（原图观看者右侧轨道）
cameraPrompt.azimuthLabelEn: object-left-front three-quarter view on the source viewer's right orbit
cameraPrompt.rollLabelZh: Roll 累计控制值 -362.00°，等效为 -2.00°，整幅画框相对原图逆时针滚转 2.00°
cameraPrompt.rollLabelEn: the cumulative roll control is -362.00 degrees, equivalent to -2.00 degrees, rotating the complete camera frame counterclockwise by 2.00 degrees relative to the source
requiredVisibleSurfaces: 目标画面必须来自锁定机位的新视锥；前景、关注对象、中景、背景、地面、环境物体和画面边界必须一起重新成像。
原图未拍到但会进入目标新视锥的对象结构与环境区域，依据原图的空间、材质、光线、色彩和画风合理想象并自然补全。
requiredOccludedSurfaces: 所有近远遮挡、入画、出画和重新显露关系必须按目标相机位置重新计算，不得沿用源图的二维遮挡顺序。
目标机位看不到的源图区域可以自然退隐或离开画面，不得为了保留原构图而复制、镜像或贴回。
perspectiveConstraints: 整幅画面的透视、视差、地平线、构图、轮廓和尺度必须共同证明相机已沿轨道移动到目标机位。
相机以固定的场景关注点为轨道圆心，镜头光轴在移动过程中持续指向同一关注点；这是环绕拍摄，不是横向平移，也不是让画面中的某个对象代替相机转动。
相机沿原图观看者右侧轨道移动，到达被摄对象自身左前方的三分之四机位；所有深度层必须出现与该水平机位一致的视差和遮挡变化。
前侧三分之四机位验收：源图中朝向镜头的主要投影必须出现明确的横向透视缩短，并与环境各深度层的视差变化一致，不得继续保持零度正面投影。
保持平视对应的自然透视，不得加入与目标俯仰矛盾的顶视或底视特征。
远景：相机真实后移并扩大环境视野，补全新进入画面的空间；不得只把源图内容二维缩小。
相机画框绕光轴逆时针滚转 2.00°；必须改变整幅画面的地平线与构图方向，不得只对主体局部做二维倾斜。
forbiddenShortcuts: 不得把任务执行成只修改关注对象而保留源图背景构图；这种结果视为失败。
不得对源图做二维旋转、镜像、卡片翻转、透视拉伸、剪贴或边缘扩图来伪装新机位。
不得复制姿态引导图的卡片、边框、底色、坐标轴、旋转环或预览外观。
cameraPrompt.deterministicPromptZh:
【相机直绘｜中文】
相机直绘版本：10.4｜双参照左右、结构化近远层映射与不可见区域补全
任务类型：相机轨道重拍（camera-orbit recapture）。场景中的人物、物品、建筑与环境属于同一个三维世界；改变的是观察相机，不是把某个对象从原背景中抠出后原地转动。
执行顺序：先围绕原图场景中心移动相机，再让镜头看回同一中心，最后从目标新视锥一次性渲染整张新照片；不得先冻结原图，再只修补局部。
左右基准（硬约束）：先按原图观看者的屏幕坐标理解左右，再换算被摄对象自身的左右；对象大致正对原相机时，这两套左右正好相反。最终以“原图画面左边/右边”作为不可歧义的相机运动基准。
原始参数：Pitch X=+7.10°，Yaw Y=-56.20°，Roll Z=-362.00°；YXZ 等效姿态为 X=+7.10°，Y=-56.20°，Z=-2.00°。
大白话机位：从原图观看者角度，把镜头沿轨道向画面右边移动 56.20°；若对象大致正对原相机，这等于来到对象自身左边；严禁走到画面左边或对象自身右边；把相机抬高到场景中心上方 7.10°，再让镜头轻微向下看回同一中心；这仍属于平视档内的轻微高机位，不要归零或夸大成明显俯拍；Roll 累计控制值 -362.00°，等效为 -2.00°，整幅画框相对原图逆时针滚转 2.00°。目标标签为对象左前方机位（原图观看者右侧轨道）、平视；四元数推导的精确方位角为 -56.20°，精确俯仰角为 +7.10°。镜头始终看回原图中的同一场景中心。
景别：远景。观察距离控制值：1.5/10。把相机明显向后移，画面关注内容的占比自然减小，并让更多原图外的环境进入画面；这不是把原图内容缩小后贴回固定背景。
画幅比例：图像 1 的原始宽高比为 1:1，目标输出尺寸为 2048x2048，必须继续保持同一 1:1 比例。不得擅自改成横图或竖图，不得拉伸、压扁、裁成其他比例或添加黑边。
景深与焦点：以原图为光学基准，识别原图真正清晰的焦点区域、前后景虚化和镜头质感；新机位下按真实拍摄距离自然更新景深，但不要无故把整张图变得全清晰，也不要抹掉原有的自然背景虚化。
空间：移动相机后重新拍摄完整场景。前景、所有对象、中景、背景、地面、墙面、天花和画面边缘全部按新机位重建透视、尺度、视差、遮挡与构图；不能冻结原背景后只转动其中的人物、物品或局部。
补全（硬约束）：画面随镜头转动，并必须一起进入目标新视锥。新视锥中原图没有拍到的对象结构和环境区域，也就是目标机位能够看到但原照片未拍到、被遮挡，或因新机位、新景别与新构图而首次进入画面的内容，都必须由模型补全：既包括新显露的对象真实结构，也包括新进入画幅的背景、地面、墙面、建筑、家具及其他环境范围。依据原图的空间关系、环境风格、材质、光线与色彩合理想象并自然补全；对象结构还必须服从已识别类别、真实构造与连接关系。不得用裁切、额外遮挡、模糊、空白、复制边缘或冻结背景逃避补全。新机位看不到的原有内容可以自然出画。
连续性：保持原图中的对象身份与结构、同一现实瞬间、环境关系、材质、颜色、光线和整体风格，但不要沿用原图的二维投影、遮挡顺序或背景排布。
视角验收：左前三分之四验收：镜头从原图观看者右边绕过去，来到对象自身左前方；不得生成观看者左边或对象自身右前方的相反视图。原来朝向镜头的投影要自然收窄，环境各深度层向正确方向产生视差。最终屏幕投影验收：镜头向原图画面右边绕行后，近层必须相对远层向最终画面左边产生视差偏移，原图中朝向零度相机的前向深度轴也必须投向最终画面左边。对象大致正对原相机时，对象自身左边的新显露近侧结构应落在最终画面右侧轮廓，而前向突出结构应延伸到最终画面左侧；“对象自身左边”绝不等于“最终画面左边”。若近层或前向结构反向投影，则说明相机实际走到了相反侧，必须判定失败并重建。仍在平视档，但必须保留精确 +7.10° 的轻微高机位和轻微向下观察；不得把角度归零，也不得夸大成明显俯拍。远景要让相机真实后退并看到更多环境，不是把原图缩小后留边。
禁止：镜像、二维旋转、卡片翻转、透视拉伸、剪贴、普通扩图，以及只改变单个对象角度而不重建整幅场景。
cameraPrompt.deterministicPromptEn:
[Direct camera recapture | English]
Camera recapture protocol: 10.4 | dual-reference left/right, structured near/far mapping, and unseen-region completion
Task type: camera-orbit recapture. The people, objects, architecture, and environment belong to one 3D world. Change the viewing camera; do not cut out and rotate one element inside a frozen source background.
Execution order: first orbit the camera around the source scene center, then aim the lens back at that same center, and finally render one complete new photograph through the target view frustum. Do not freeze the source image and patch only a local region.
Left/right reference (hard constraint): interpret left and right first in the source viewer's screen coordinates, then translate them into the depicted object's own coordinates. For an object approximately facing the source camera, these two left/right systems are opposite. The source frame's left or right edge is the unambiguous camera-motion reference.
Raw controls: Pitch X=+7.10 degrees, Yaw Y=-56.20 degrees, Roll Z=-362.00 degrees. The equivalent YXZ pose is X=+7.10 degrees, Y=-56.20 degrees, Z=-2.00 degrees.
Plain-language camera position: from the source viewer's perspective, orbit the camera 56.20 degrees toward the RIGHT edge of the source frame; for an object approximately facing the source camera, this places the camera on the object's own LEFT; do not move toward the source frame's left edge or the object's own right; raise the camera 7.10 degrees above the scene center and look slightly downward toward that same center; this remains a subtle elevated position within the eye-level band, so do not round it to zero or exaggerate it into an obvious high-angle shot; the cumulative roll control is -362.00 degrees, equivalent to -2.00 degrees, rotating the complete camera frame counterclockwise by 2.00 degrees relative to the source. Target label: object-left-front three-quarter view on the source viewer's right orbit, eye level. Quaternion-derived azimuth is -56.20 degrees and elevation is +7.10 degrees. Keep the lens aimed at the same scene center as in the source image.
Shot size: wide shot. Camera-distance control: 1.5/10. Move the camera clearly backward so the depicted focus occupies less of the frame and more previously unseen environment enters the image. Do not merely shrink the source subject inside a frozen background.
Frame and aspect ratio: image 1 has a source aspect ratio of 1:1; render exactly 2048x2048 with the same 1:1 ratio. Do not change it into a landscape or portrait canvas, stretch it, squash it, crop it to another ratio, or add letterboxing.
Depth of field and focus: use the source image as the optical reference. Identify its true focal plane, sharp region, foreground/background blur, and lens character. Update depth of field naturally for the new camera distance, but do not make the entire image uniformly sharp or erase the source's natural background separation.
Space: move the camera and recapture the complete scene. Rebuild the perspective, scale, parallax, occlusion, and composition of the foreground, every object, midground, background, ground, walls, ceiling, and frame edges from the new camera position. Do not freeze the source background and rotate only a person, object, or local region.
Completion (hard constraint): the whole image moves with the camera and must enter the target view frustum. Infer and naturally complete every object structure and environment region that enters the new view frustum but was not captured in the source. This includes everything absent from the source photograph, previously occluded, or newly entering the frame because of the target camera position, shot size, and composition: both newly revealed real object structures and newly framed background, ground, walls, architecture, furniture, and other environment areas. Infer them conservatively from the source spatial relationships, environment style, materials, lighting, and colors; object structures must also follow the detected category, real construction, and connection logic. Do not evade completion with cropping, extra occluders, blur, blank areas, duplicated edges, or a frozen source background. Source content outside the target view may naturally leave the frame.
Continuity: retain the identities and structures of the depicted elements, the same real-world moment, environment relationships, materials, colors, lighting, and overall style. Recompute the 2D projection, occlusion order, and background layout from the target camera instead of reusing those from the source image.
Acceptance: For the object-left-front three-quarter view, orbit toward the RIGHT edge of the source frame and arrive on the object's own left-front side. Do not produce the opposite source-frame-left or object-right-front view. Source-facing projections must narrow naturally and every depth layer must show parallax in the correct direction. Final screen-projection audit: after the camera orbits toward the RIGHT edge of the source frame, near depth layers must shift toward the LEFT side of the final image relative to far layers, and the source-facing forward depth axis must also project toward the final image's LEFT. When the depicted object approximately faces the source camera, newly revealed near structures on the object's own left belong on the RIGHT contour of the final image, while forward-projecting structures extend toward the final image's LEFT. The object's own left never means the final image's left. If these projections reverse, the camera reached the opposite side; reject and rebuild. This remains in the eye-level band, but preserve the exact +7.10 degrees subtle elevated camera position and slight downward look. Do not round it to zero or exaggerate it into an obvious high-angle shot. For the wide shot, physically move the camera back and reveal more environment; do not merely shrink the source image and add borders.
Reject: mirroring, 2D rotation, card flipping, perspective warping, cut-and-paste, ordinary outpainting, or changing only one object's angle without rebuilding the complete scene.

renderPrompt:
[Direct camera recapture | English]
Camera recapture protocol: 10.4 | dual-reference left/right, structured near/far mapping, and unseen-region completion
Task type: camera-orbit recapture. The people, objects, architecture, and environment belong to one 3D world. Change the viewing camera; do not cut out and rotate one element inside a frozen source background.
Execution order: first orbit the camera around the source scene center, then aim the lens back at that same center, and finally render one complete new photograph through the target view frustum. Do not freeze the source image and patch only a local region.

Shot size: wide shot. Camera-distance control: 1.5/10. Move the camera clearly backward so the depicted focus occupies less of the frame and more previously unseen environment enters the image. Do not merely shrink the source subject inside a frozen background.

Depth of field and focus: use the source image as the optical reference. Identify its true focal plane, sharp region, foreground/background blur, and lens character. Space: move the camera and recapture the complete scene. Rebuild the perspective, scale, parallax, occlusion, and composition of the foreground, every object, midground, background, ground, walls, ceiling, and frame edges from the new camera position. Do not freeze the source background and rotate only a person, object, or local region.
Completion (hard constraint): the whole image moves with the camera and must enter the target view frustum. Infer and naturally complete every object structure and environment region that enters the new view frustum but was not captured in the source. This includes everything absent from the source photograph, previously occluded, or newly entering the frame because of the target camera position, shot size, and composition: both newly revealed real object structures and newly framed background, ground, walls, architecture, furniture, and other environment areas. Infer them conservatively from the source spatial relationships, environment style, materials, lighting, and colors; object structures must also follow the detected category, real construction, and connection logic. Do not evade completion with cropping, extra occluders, blur, blank areas, duplicated edges, or a frozen source background. Source content outside the target view may naturally leave the frame.

Recompute the 2D projection, occlusion order, and background layout from the target camera instead of reusing those from the source image.
Acceptance: For the object-left-front three-quarter view, orbit toward the RIGHT edge of the source frame and arrive on the object's own left-front side. Do not produce the opposite source-frame-left or object-right-front view. Source-facing projections must narrow naturally and every depth layer must show parallax in the correct direction. Final screen-projection audit: after the camera orbits toward the RIGHT edge of the source frame, near depth layers must shift toward the LEFT side of the final image relative to far layers, and the source-facing forward depth axis must also project toward the final image's LEFT. When the depicted object approximately faces the source camera, newly revealed near structures on the object's own left belong on the RIGHT contour of the final image, while forward-projecting structures extend toward the final image's LEFT. The object's own left never means the final image's left. If these projections reverse, the camera reached the opposite side; reject and rebuild. This remains in the eye-level band, but preserve the exact +7.10 degrees subtle elevated camera position and slight downward look. Do not round it to zero or exaggerate it into an obvious high-angle shot. For the wide shot, physically move the camera back and reveal more environment; do not merely shrink the source image and add borders.
Reject: mirroring, 2D rotation, card flipping, perspective warping, cut-and-paste, ordinary outpainting, or changing only one object's angle without rebuilding the complete scene.
Inputs: image 1 is the factual reference for the depicted elements and complete environment. Image 2 is the rotated target-camera guide and only indicates camera direction, roll, composition, and shot size; do not copy its card, border, background color, or preview appearance.
[gpt-5.6-sol visual facts | subordinate to the locked camera block]
Detected category: person.
Identity, structure, material, lighting, and style facts:
- Natural photorealistic indoor portrait of a young East Asian woman with gray-brown eyes, dark brown-black hair in a loose bun, wispy strands along her cheeks and neck, and a lightweight textured white square-neck top. Preserve her facial identity and hairstyle, delicate skin, subtle makeup, and pink lips. Soft window light illuminates her face; the background contains a pale window frame, beige-gray wall, and warm brown wooden furniture. Keep the eyes and face sharply focused with natural background blur.
- Preserve the young East Asian woman’s oval face, fine straight brows, gray-brown eyes, small nose, soft jawline, and naturally pink lip shape.
- Preserve the dark brown-black loose high bun, slightly off-center hairline part, fine temple strands on both sides, and loose neck hairs.
- Preserve delicate natural skin, subtle warm eye makeup, no jewelry, no glasses, and a calm gentle expression.
- Preserve the lightweight white square-neck top and its fine woven texture, adding no text, patterns, or logos.
Conservative completion plan for newly visible structures:
- Complete the rounded hair volume from her anatomical left rear skull into the back of the bun, using overlapping locks and a few loose strands consistent with the existing dark hair.
- Complete the hairline, temple, and neck connection behind her anatomical left ear with natural skin transitions and fine wispy hairs.
- Complete her anatomical left shoulder cap, upper arm, and sleeve edge as one continuous structure, retaining the white thin fabric, soft folds, and square-neck connection.
- If additional lower space becomes visible, conservatively continue the upper chest, both shoulders, and white top without inventing jewelry, hands, or extra clothing layers.
Complete environment continuity plan:
- Keep the foreground clean without new obstructions; the eyes and face remain the crisp midground focal area with a soft high-resolution portrait rendering.
- Continue the pale window frame and cool gray-green vertical recess at screen left, plus the beige-gray wall and warm brown wooden furniture at screen right, all with pronounced natural blur.
- Any newly included environment should extend the existing wall, window-side structure, furniture surfaces, and soft shadows without copying borders or leaving empty areas.
- The floor is not recorded; if lower space enters the image, conservatively add a low-detail neutral indoor floor and soft shadow beneath furniture while preserving the square aspect ratio.
Category-specific projection evidence:
- Her anatomical left ear, left cheekbone-to-jaw contour, and the left attachment of the bun should become more fully visible, corresponding to structures on the original screen right.
- Her anatomical right eye, right cheek, and right shoulder correspond to the original screen left and should appear moderately shortened and partly overlapped by the nose, facial front edge, and head contour.
- The nasal bridge, tip, lips, and chin must remain naturally continuous; both eyes stay identifiable with unchanged gray-brown irises, natural lashes, and subtle peach eye makeup.
- The white top’s square neckline, clavicle area, and shoulder connections must remain anatomically coherent, with visible lightweight woven texture and soft folds.
Category-specific occlusion evidence:
- The nose may slightly overlap her anatomical right inner eye corner and right cheek, but must not incorrectly cover her anatomical left eye.
- Wispy temple hair may partially cross her anatomical left ear, while the helix, lobe, and connection to the head remain coherent.
- The bun overlaps the rear head contour, and loose strands may only partly cross the side of the neck without becoming an unnatural solid hair curtain.
- Her anatomical right shoulder and right neckline appear narrower through depth overlap and must not be exchanged with the left shoulder.
Uncertain details must use the most conservative completion:
- The back of the bun, her anatomical left upper arm, and the off-frame floor are not directly recorded in Image 1 and require minimal-detail continuous completion.
- The warm brown background object is identifiable as wooden furniture, but its exact type and full shape are uncertain, so distinct drawers, handles, or decorations should not be invented.
These visual facts may not redefine the camera, shot size, roll, framing, or target projection.
Execution: move the virtual camera to the locked orbit position, then recapture the entire scene through the new view frustum. Treat image 1 as scene evidence, not as a frozen background. Reproject every depicted element and the environment from the new camera position.
Mandatory unseen-area completion: reconstruct all real object structures that become visible from the target camera but were absent or occluded in image 1, and generate every environment area that newly enters the target frame. Infer these areas conservatively from image 1's category, geometry, spatial layout, materials, lighting, color, and style. Cropping, blur, blank space, duplicated borders, extra occluders, or a frozen source background are not valid substitutes.
Direction audit: use the source frame's left/right direction as the primary check. The object's own left/right is the opposite only when it approximately faces the source camera. Reject any result whose camera arrives on the opposite side.
Additional requirement: Continue the same real-world moment, spatial relationships, materials, lighting, colors, and visual style without adding unrelated concepts.
Output: return one clean, high-fidelity image photographed from the specified camera position. Do not show axes, rotation rings, guides, captions, or watermarks.
optimizedPrompt: Natural photorealistic indoor portrait of a young East Asian woman with gray-brown eyes, dark brown-black hair in a loose bun, wispy strands along her cheeks and neck, and a lightweight textured white square-neck top. Preserve her facial identity and hairstyle, delicate skin, subtle makeup, and pink lips. Soft window light illuminates her face; the background contains a pale window frame, beige-gray wall, and warm brown wooden furniture. Keep the eyes and face sharply focused with natural background blur.
viewDescription: Natural photorealistic indoor portrait of a young East Asian woman with gray-brown eyes, dark brown-black hair in a loose bun, wispy strands along her cheeks and neck, and a lightweight textured white square-neck top. Preserve her facial identity and hairstyle, delicate skin, subtle makeup, and pink lips. Soft window light illuminates her face; the background contains a pale window frame, beige-gray wall, and warm brown wooden furniture. Keep the eyes and face sharply focused with natural background blur.
sourceViewDescription: Image 1 is a square close photorealistic portrait with the woman roughly facing the original camera; screen right corresponds to her anatomical left, where her left ear, cheek, and shoulder are clearer, while a pale window frame sits at screen left and warm brown furniture is behind her at screen right.
targetViewDescription: object-left-front three-quarter view on the source viewer's right orbit, eye level, wide shot, the cumulative roll control is -362.00 degrees, equivalent to -2.00 degrees, rotating the complete camera frame counterclockwise by 2.00 degrees relative to the source
relativeCameraMotion: The camera moves from the source zero-degree reference to the server-locked target pose, and the complete scene is reprojected through the new view frustum.
visibilityConstraints: Her anatomical left ear, left cheekbone-to-jaw contour, and the left attachment of the bun should become more fully visible, corresponding to structures on the original screen right.

Her anatomical right eye, right cheek, and right shoulder correspond to the original screen left and should appear moderately shortened and partly overlapped by the nose, facial front edge, and head contour.
The nasal bridge, tip, lips, and chin must remain naturally continuous; both eyes stay identifiable with unchanged gray-brown irises, natural lashes, and subtle peach eye makeup.
The white top’s square neckline, clavicle area, and shoulder connections must remain anatomically coherent, with visible lightweight woven texture and soft folds.
occlusionConstraints: The nose may slightly overlap her anatomical right inner eye corner and right cheek, but must not incorrectly cover her anatomical left eye.
Wispy temple hair may partially cross her anatomical left ear, while the helix, lobe, and connection to the head remain coherent.
The bun overlaps the rear head contour, and loose strands may only partly cross the side of the neck without becoming an unnatural solid hair curtain.
